name: Greptile
pricing_notes: Free tier (10 repos); Pro $40/mo; Enterprise custom; API pay-per-query

auth.notes: API key + GitHub/GitLab OAuth for repo indexing; Bearer token for queries

performance.notes: First query on new repo triggers indexing (30s-5min); subsequent queries 1-5s depending on codebase size

agent_readiness.notes: Codebase understanding API — index GitHub/GitLab repos, then query in natural language. Returns code references with file paths and line numbers. Excellent for agents that need to understand unfamiliar codebases. Streaming responses supported. Simple REST API with Python/JS SDKs.

gotchas: First-time repo indexing latency is high (minutes for large repos)
Answers can hallucinate if codebase context is insufficient
No self-hosted option — all code sent to Greptile servers
Rate limits can be hit quickly in agentic loops querying frequently
best_when: Agents that need to navigate and understand large unfamiliar codebases
Code review bots that need semantic understanding of changes
Developer tools needing 'ask your codebase' capability
CI/CD pipelines answering questions about code during review
avoid_when: Security-sensitive codebases that cannot leave premises
Teams with simple codebases (overkill vs. grep/AST tools)
summary: Greptile indexes GitHub/GitLab repositories and provides a natural-language query API for code understanding. Agents can ask 'how does authentication work?' and receive answers with cited file paths and line numbers. Simple REST API with streaming. Best for agents navigating large codebases. Not suitable for security-sensitive code that must stay on-premises.
